User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in the CineCapture application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

email.description: User's email address for login and communication.

passwordHash.description: Hashed password for secure authentication.  Note:  This application will use bcrypt.

username.description: User's chosen username or display name.

profileImageUrl.description: URL of the user's profile image.

registrationDate.description: Date and time when the user registered.

isAdmin.description: Indicates whether the user is an administrator.

Content.title: Content
Content.description: Represents a movie, series, or mini-series recommended by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the content item.

userId.description: Reference to the User who recommended this content. (Relationship: User 1:N Content)

title.description: Title of the movie or series.

summary.description: A brief summary or synopsis of the content.

posterUrl.description: URL of the content's poster image.

status.description: User's status for this content (e.g., 'watched', 'unwatched', 'in progress').

tags.description: Keywords or categories associated with the content.

dateAdded.description: Date when the content was added to the user's library.

Capture.title: Capture
Capture.description: Represents a screenshot uploaded by a user for content analysis.

userId.description: Reference to the User who uploaded the capture. (Relationship: User 1:N Capture)

contentId.description: Reference to the Content extracted from the capture. (Relationship: Content 1:N Capture)

uploadDate.description: Date when the capture was uploaded.

Genre.title: Genre
Genre.description: Represents a genre associated with a content item.

ContentGenre.title: ContentGenre
ContentGenre.description: Join table for the many-to-many relationship between Content and Genre.

contentId.description: Reference to Content. (Relationship: Content N:N Genre)

genreId.description: Reference to Genre. (Relationship: Content N:N Genre)

Note.title: Note
Note.description: Represents a user's note or comment on a content item.

contentId.description: Reference to the Content being reviewed. (Relationship: Content 1:N Note)

rating.description: User's rating for the content (e.g., on a scale of 1-5).

comment.description: User's comments or review of the content.

date.description: Date when the note was created.

definition.description: Stores user profile information.  Access restricted to the user and administrators.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores content items recommended by a specific user. Path-based ownership ensures that only the user can access this data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the content item.

definition.description: Stores screenshots uploaded by a user for content analysis. Nested within the content to maintain the 1:N relationship.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the content item.

params.name: captureId
params.description: The unique identifier for the capture.

definition.description: Stores notes and comments created by the user related to content. Nested within the content to maintain the 1:N relationship.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the content item.

params.name: noteId
params.description: The unique identifier for the note.

definition.description: Stores globally available genre types. Read access is public; write access is restricted to admins.

params.name: genreId
params.description: The unique identifier for the genre.

definition.description: Stores the relationship between Content and Genre.  Allows genres to be associated with content items.

params.name: contentGenreId
params.description: The unique identifier for the content genre relationship.

definition.description: Marks a user as an administrator. The presence of a document at this path grants admin privileges. (Existence over Content)

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed to support the CineCapture application's core features, focusing on secure user authentication and personalized content libraries. The structure prioritizes authorization independence and efficient data retrieval, especially when listing content.

*   **Users Collection (`/users/{userId}`):** Stores user profiles, including authentication data (email, password hash) and profile information. Path-based ownership ensures that only the authenticated user or an admin can access a user's profile.
*   **Content Collection (`/users/{userId}/content/{contentId}`):** Each user has their own subcollection of `content`. This structure enforces path-based ownership, ensuring that only the user who owns the content or an admin can access it. This approach guarantees that users can only see their own content library and isolates data silos for each user. There are no authorization dependencies between different users.
*   **Roles Collection (`/roles_admin/{userId}`):** An administrator role is created using the 'Existence over Content' method for role management. The existence of a document in `/roles_admin/{userId}` grants administrative privileges to the user with the corresponding `userId`. This design enables easy role assignment and verification in security rules without needing to read the document's content.

**Authorization Independence (Strategy A):** The core of the authorization model relies on path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/content/{contentId}`). This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, allowing atomic operations and improving rule complexity.

**Structural Segregation (Strategy B):** Data with different access needs are stored in separate collections. User profiles are stored in `/users/{userId}`, ensuring private access, while admin roles are managed in `/roles_admin/{userId}`.  Each user's content is stored in their own subcollection `users/{userId}/content/{contentId}`, ensuring that users only have access to their own content. This simplifies security rules by enforcing consistent access control within each collection.

**Access Modeling (Strategy C):** Path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/content/{contentId}`) is used for user-owned content. The `userId` in the path directly corresponds to the authenticated user's `uid`, simplifying the rules.  The admin role leverages the 'Existence over Content' pattern.

**QAPs Support:**

*   **Secure List Operations:** The segregation of user data into separate subcollections (`/users/{userId}/content/{contentId}`) allows secure list operations. Security rules can easily verify that the `request.auth.uid` matches the `userId` in the path, ensuring users can only list content they own.

This structure provides a secure and scalable foundation for the CineCapture application, aligning with the core design principles and strategies outlined in the prompt.
